differential_diagnoses: Pyogenic arthritis, pyoderma gangrenosum, and acne (PAPA) syndrome
next_steps: Incisional skin biopsy of the edge of an ulcer for histopathology and tissue culture
Screening for underlying inflammatory bowel disease (colonoscopy)
Continued supportive care and monitoring for multisystem organ involvement
Re-evaluation of immunosuppressive treatment options if steroids are ineffective

differential_diagnoses: PASH syndrome (Pyoderma gangrenosum, Acne, and Suppurative Hidradenitis)
PAPA syndrome (Pyogenic Arthritis, Pyoderma gangrenosum, and Acne)
Disseminated Crohn's disease with cutaneous manifestations
Septic emboli from occult source (e.g., endocarditis or pelvic thrombophlebitis)
next_steps: Perform diagnostic skin biopsy (edge of ulcer) for histopathology and tissue/fungal/mycobacterial culture
Initiate consultation with Dermatology and Rheumatology for urgent management of suspected neutrophilic dermatosis
Consult Infectious Disease to finalize exclusion of occult deep-seated infection (including repeat blood cultures and fungal/viral workup)
Initiate systemic corticosteroids (e.g., methylprednisolone) once infection is reasonably excluded
Perform ophthalmologic evaluation to rule out uveitis or other signs of Behçet's disease
Consider genetic testing for PSTPIP1 gene mutations
Perform colonoscopy to investigate potential IBD/Crohn's disease
Serial cardiovascular monitoring including repeat echocardiogram or cardiac MRI to assess for inflammatory myocardial involvement
